case_title: DWAYNE GROSS VS. MAJID JAZAYERI ET AL

docket_entries.description: DECLARATION OF MARK CARBONE IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANTS MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES FROM THE COMPLAINT AND EXHIBIT A (TRANSACTION ID # 58259229) FILED BY DEFENDANT JAZAYERI, MAJID

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MAJID JAZAYERI'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT RELIES ON BAD LAW FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT GROSS NEGLIGENCE DOES NOT WARRANT PUNITIVE DAMAGES. "[A] CONSCIOUS DISREGARD OF THE SAFETY OF OTHERS MAY CONSTITUTE MALICE WITHIN THE MEANING OF [CC 3294]." (TAYLOR V. SUPER. CT. (1979) 24 CAL.3D 890, 895-96; SEE ALSO IN RE PLYAM ((B.A.P. 9TH CIR. 2015) 530 B.R. 456, 465 ["PREVIOUSLY, SOME CALIFORNIA COURTS HELD THAT RECKLESS CONDUCT DID NOT ESTABLISH MALICE AS REQUIRED FOR A PUNITIVE DAMAGES AWARD. SEE . . . EBAUGH V. RABKIN [1972], 22 CAL.APP.3D 891, 896"].) PLAINTIFF PROPERLY ASSERTS THAT IT MAY BE MALICIOUS TO ALLEGEDLY HIT A PEDESTRIAN IN A CROSSWALK AND LEAVE THE SCENE WITHOUT INQUIRY. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: GISELLE CASEY, CSR #8098. (302/EG)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-07-2016 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-11-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MAJID JAZAYERI'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT - Defendant relies on bad law for the proposition that gross negligence does not warrant punitive damages. "[A] conscious disregard of the safety of others may constitute malice within the meaning of [CC 3294]." (Taylor v. Super. Ct. (1979) 24 Cal.3d 890, 895-96; see also In re Plyam ((B.A.P. 9th Cir. 2015) 530 B.R. 456, 465 ["Previously, some California courts held that reckless conduct did not establish malice as required for a punitive damages award. See . . . Ebaugh v. Rabkin [1972], 22 Cal.App.3d 891, 896"].) Plaintiff properly asserts that it may be malicious to allegedly hit a pedestrian in a crosswalk and leave the scene without inquiry. Order signed.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. (302/EG)
